channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: UnitedHealthcare taught us ways to deny claims: Former employee
item.description: Collins described crying at her desk while handling calls from desperate patients, as supervisors laughed.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: At least 15 people injured after Florida train hits fire truck
item.description: Three firefighters and 12 Brightline passengers were injured Saturday morning following a collision between a Delray Beach Fire Rescue truck and a train.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, KREX 5

item.title: Big Lots reaches deal to keep hundreds of US stores open
item.description: The discount chain Big Lots, which filed for bankruptcy protection in September, has reached a deal that will keep hundreds of its stores and distribution centers open. Big Lots said Friday it will be sold to Gordon Brothers Retail Partners, a firm that specializes in distressed companies.
item.dc:creator: DEE-ANN DURBIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Abortions are up in the US. It's a complicated picture as women turn to pills, travel
item.description: Even with abortion bans in place in most Republican-controlled states, the number of people obtaining them has grown slightly.
item.dc:creator: Geoff Mulvihill And Kevin S. Vineys, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Olivia Hussey, star of 1968 film 'Romeo and Juliet,' dies at 73
item.description: Olivia Hussey, the actor who starred as a teenage Juliet in the 1968 film “Romeo and Juliet,” has died, her family said on social media Saturday. She was 73.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Winning $1.22 billion Mega Millions ticket sold in California
item.description: One lucky Californian is the state's newest billionaire after winning numbers for the $1.22 billion Mega Millions jackpot were drawn Friday night. The winning numbers were 3, 7, 37, 49, 55 and the Mega Ball number was 6. The Megaplier was 3X.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Chow, KTLA 5

item.title: Walmart delivery driver caught pleasuring himself in Horry County woman's driveway after dropping off groceries, lawsuit claims
item.description: HORRY COUNTY, S.C. (WBTW) -- A woman has filed a lawsuit in Horry County, South Carolina alleging that she caught a Walmart delivery driver pleasuring himself inside his vehicle in her driveway after dropping off her groceries.
item.dc:creator: Jordan White, NewsNation

item.title: Judge signals that contempt hearing for Rudy Giuliani over his assets might not go well for him
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A federal judge is signaling that Rudy Giuliani’s contempt hearing next Friday might not end so well for the former New York City mayor and onetime personal lawyer for President-elect Donald Trump as two Georgia election poll workers try to collect a $148 million defamation award the
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Explosion at Tyson Foods plant leaves 1 dead, others injured
item.description: An explosion at a Tyson Foods plant in Georgia late Thursday night left one person dead and others injured.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, Fox 5

item.title: Sportscasting icon Greg Gumbel dies at 78
item.description: Sportscaster Greg Gumbel, best known for his long career with CBS Sports, has died Friday, Dec. 27, according to CBS Sports and Gumbel's family.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Man indicted in burning death of woman inside a New York City subway train, prosecutors say
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The man accused of burning a sleeping woman to death inside a New York City subway train has been indicted on murder and arson charges, prosecutors announced Friday, as authorities continue working to identify the victim.
item.dc:creator: CEDAR ATTANASIO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Pitbull helps install final crystal triangles on Times Square ball ahead of New Year's Eve
item.description: The last of the crystal triangles that make up this year's Times Square New Year's Eve ball were installed on Friday morning, marking the first time in 10 years that all 2,688 were replaced at once.
item.dc:creator: BY MELISSA GOLDIN, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Economist says mass deportations will cost Mexico tens of billions of dollars, a blow to its economy
item.description: "It's going to be a blow just in terms of the number of people sent here, but what about all those countries that refuse to take in their own people, they will likely end up in Mexico, you have to care for them as well," he said.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, Fox 5

item.title: US homelessness up 18% as affordable housing remains out of reach for many people
item.description: The United States saw an 18.1% increase in homelessness this year, a dramatic rise driven mostly by a lack of affordable housing as well as devastating natural disasters and a surge of migrants in several parts of the country, federal officials said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Michael Casey, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Another unticketed passenger attempts to stow away on a Delta flight
item.description: In the thick of holiday travel, another unticketed passenger attempted to stow away on a Delta Air Lines flight.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Bird flu virus in Louisiana patient likely mutated to be more transmissible, CDC says
item.description: The agency, in its latest update released Thursday, said the mutations were not found in samples taken from wild birds, suggesting the virus changed over the course of the patient’s infection.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, ABC 4

item.title: More than a third of Americans racked up over $1,000 in debt this holiday season: report
item.description: (KRON) -- More than one third of Americans racked up over $1,000 in credit card debt this holiday season, according to a new report. The report from LendingTree indicates that 36% of Americans took on an average of $1,181 in debt, up from $1,028 in 2023.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: Man indicted in burning death of woman inside New York City subway train: prosecutors
item.description: The indictment comes days after Sebastian Zapeta’s arrest and subsequent police questioning, in which authorities say he claimed not to know what had happened but identified himself in photos and surveillance video showing the fire being lit.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Most blame insurance profits and denials alongside the killer in UHC CEO death: poll
item.description: Most Americans believe health insurance profits and coverage denials share responsibility for the killing of UnitedHealthcare's CEO — although not as much as the person who pulled the trigger, according to a new poll.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: These movies are some of 2025's most anticipated
item.description: With the vast majority of 2024 in the rearview mirror, it's time to start looking at the most anticipated films primed to take the silver screen in 2025. Full of adaptations, sequels, and reboots of beloved properties, viewers will not have difficulty finding something familiar to enjoy.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Massachusetts family found dead from carbon monoxide exposure
item.description: A family from Newton, Massachusetts, have been identified as the victims found dead inside a home on Christmas Day after an apparent exposure to carbon monoxide.
item.dc:creator: Melanie DaSilva, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Mexico tests app for migrants to send alert if they're about to be detained in US
item.description: U.S. authorities are obliged to give notice to home-country consulates when a foreign citizen is detained. Mexico says it has beefed up consular staff and legal aid to help migrants in the legal process related to deportation.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Former NCAA quarterback Tommy Lazzaro dies in 'tragic' accident at US Air Force base
item.description: Tommy Lazzaro, an Army sergeant and former quarterback for the Central Michigan Chippewas, has died following an accident at Eglin Air Force Base in Florida. He was 27.
item.dc:creator: Matt Jaworowski, KRON4

item.title: Get off my lawn! 5 times squatters took advantage of unwitting homeowners in 2024
item.description: A string of high-profile squatter cases have pushed multiple states to pass legislation to protect homeowners this year.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Retailers, chains that announced closures in 2024
item.description: It was another tough year for restaurants and traditional brick-and-mortar retailers. Several national chains announced closures and bankruptcies in 2024 – not to mention the local small businesses that quietly shuttered around the country.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, KRON4

item.title: Richard Parsons, prominent executive who led Time Warner and Citigroup, dies at 76
item.description: Richard Parsons, one of corporate America’s most prominent Black executives who held top posts at Time Warner and Citigroup, has died.
item.dc:creator: Anick Jesdanun And Michael R. Sisak, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Powerful thunderstorms rumble across Texas, delaying holiday travel
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — Some flights were delayed or canceled in Texas on Thursday after a line of thunderstorms started moving across parts of the state in a system the National Weather Service predicted could bring high winds, hail and possible tornadoes.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Boy killed in Colorado reservation shooting was in bed when he was hit
item.description: DENVER (AP) — A 7-year-old boy who died in a shooting that left 24 bullet holes in a home on a tribal reservation in Colorado was lying next to his father in bed when he was hit, according to court documents unsealed Thursday.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Florida woman arrested after allegedly breaking in, stabbing person over pizza delivery tip
item.description: Armed with a knife, the suspect began attacking one of the victims, allegedly upset about the tip she received during an earlier delivery, deputies said.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, KRON4
